explanation: The command uses quotes around the file paths to safely handle spaces and special characters. This is a valid mutation technique (insertion of quotes) that does not alter the behavior of the original command.
This is the original command with no mutation applied. It is included for completeness and as a baseline for comparison.

The command is restructured using short path notation (e.g., C:\Progra~1 instead of C:\Program Files) to test path normalization. However, in this case, the paths do not contain spaces, so no change is needed.
The command uses substitution of long options with short options where applicable. In this case, no such substitution is needed as the command is already minimal.
The command omits optional elements such as verbose flags (-f, -v) while preserving the core behavior of encoding a file.
